tools.name: GitHub branch protection (built-in)
tools.label: GitHub branch protection (built-in)
tools.changesWhen: Compliance requires stricter review count or signed commits
tools.whenToUse: Every repo deploying to production
tools.whenNot: Never skip for prod repos
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs GitLab protected branches: GitHub has fine-grained token control; GitLab has more granular merge approval counts

tools.name: GitHub CODEOWNERS (built-in)
tools.label: GitHub CODEOWNERS (built-in)
tools.changesWhen: Team ownership changes; new service paths added
tools.whenToUse: Always — auto-assigns reviewers on paths containing secrets config, IaC, or auth code
tools.whenNot: Never skip for security-critical paths
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs manual reviewer assignment: CODEOWNERS auto-assigns; manual assignment requires each PR author to know the owner

tools.name: GitHub Actions OIDC (built-in)
tools.label: GitHub Actions OIDC (built-in)
tools.changesWhen: Cloud provider migration; IAM role ARN or trust policy changes
tools.ciIntegration: GitHub native — permissions: id-token: write in workflow
tools.whenToUse: Always — eliminates long-lived static credentials from CI
tools.whenNot: Use stored secrets only when the cloud provider does not support OIDC federation
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs static secrets: OIDC tokens expire per job; static secrets persist until manually rotated

tools.name: GHCR — GitHub Container Registry
tools.label: GHCR — GitHub Container Registry
tools.changesWhen: Org transfer; visibility policy changes
tools.whenToUse: Default for GitHub-hosted repos — free private registry, tight OIDC integration
tools.whenNot: Use ECR when AWS VPC endpoint access is required by compliance
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs ECR: GHCR needs no AWS account; ECR has deeper IAM and VPC endpoint policy control

tools.name: ECR — Elastic Container Registry
tools.label: ECR — Elastic Container Registry
tools.license: AWS — pay per GB stored + transferred
tools.changesWhen: AWS account migration; ECR lifecycle policy changes
tools.whenToUse: AWS workloads requiring VPC endpoint registry access or cross-account pull policies
tools.whenNot: Skip when not on AWS
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: ECR has deeper AWS IAM integration; GHCR needs no AWS account

tools.name: Kyverno v1.13
tools.label: Kyverno v1.13
tools.changesWhen: New image registries added; policy rules updated; new namespaces scoped
tools.whenToUse: Any Kubernetes cluster — blocks unsigned images at admission before the pod starts
tools.whenNot: Skip for non-Kubernetes deployments (serverless, VMs)
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs OPA/Gatekeeper: Kyverno policies are plain Kubernetes YAML; Gatekeeper requires Rego language

tools.name: Renovate v43
tools.label: Renovate v43
tools.changesWhen: Automerge rules change; grouping rules updated; new registries added
tools.ciIntegration: GitHub Action or self-hosted Renovate bot
tools.whenToUse: Multiple repos or monorepos needing grouped, automerge-capable dependency updates
tools.whenNot: Use Dependabot when zero-config GitHub-native updates are sufficient
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Dependabot: Renovate has grouping, automerge, and multi-registry support; Dependabot needs no config file

tools.name: Dependabot (built-in)
tools.label: Dependabot (built-in)
tools.changesWhen: Ignore rules change; schedule changes
tools.whenToUse: Simple single-ecosystem repos where Renovate config overhead is not justified
tools.whenNot: Replace with Renovate for monorepos or multi-ecosystem repos
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Renovate: Dependabot needs zero setup; Renovate needs renovate.json but gives far more control

tools.name: SonarLint (VS Code / JetBrains)
tools.label: SonarLint (VS Code / JetBrains)
tools.changesWhen: Connected mode: SonarQube/Cloud ruleset updates
tools.whenToUse: Any developer wanting real-time SAST feedback without waiting for CI
tools.whenNot: Skip for editors without SonarLint support
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs CodeQL IDE extension: SonarLint works in VS Code and JetBrains; CodeQL IDE is VS Code only

tools.name: hadolint IDE extension
tools.label: hadolint IDE extension
tools.changesWhen: hadolint ruleset updates
tools.whenToUse: Any developer writing Dockerfiles — catches FROM, RUN, and layer issues inline
tools.whenNot: Skip for non-container workloads
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs manual Dockerfile review: hadolint checks all instructions automatically; manual review misses subtle layer ordering issues

tools.name: pre-commit v3.8 (framework)
tools.label: pre-commit v3.8 (framework)
tools.changesWhen: Hooks added or removed; .pre-commit-config.yaml changes
tools.whenToUse: Every repo — manages all hooks in one config file, runs on git commit
tools.whenNot: Never skip on repos with a CI gate
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs husky: pre-commit supports all languages; husky is Node.js-only

tools.name: gitleaks v8.21
tools.label: gitleaks v8.21
tools.changesWhen: Custom regex patterns added for org-specific secret formats
tools.whenToUse: Every repo — blocks commit if a secret pattern is detected in staged files
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs truffleHog: gitleaks is faster with deterministic regex rules; truffleHog adds entropy-based detection for unknown formats

tools.name: hadolint v2.12
tools.label: hadolint v2.12
tools.changesWhen: Best practice rule updates
tools.whenToUse: Any repo containing Dockerfiles
tools.whenNot: Skip for repos with no Dockerfiles
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs dockerfile-lint: hadolint is actively maintained with more rules; dockerfile-lint is unmaintained

tools.name: checkov v3
tools.label: checkov v3
tools.changesWhen: New IaC resource types added; policy framework updates
tools.ciIntegration: Run as pre-commit hook via bridgecrewio/checkov
tools.whenToUse: Any repo with IaC files
tools.whenNot: Skip for repos with no IaC
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs KICS: checkov has broader multi-framework support; KICS has deeper Terraform-specific rule coverage

tools.name: gitleaks v8.21
tools.label: gitleaks v8.21
tools.changesWhen: Custom rules added; .gitleaks.toml baseline updated
tools.whenToUse: Every repo — runs on staged files at git commit, blocks before push
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs truffleHog: gitleaks is faster for pre-commit use; truffleHog v3 scans full repo history with entropy detection

tools.name: pre-commit v3.8
tools.label: pre-commit v3.8
tools.changesWhen: Hook config changes; new hooks added to .pre-commit-config.yaml
tools.whenToUse: Every PR — re-runs hooks in CI to catch bypassed local hooks (--no-verify)
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs running hooks individually: pre-commit manages all hooks in one config; individual runs require separate CI steps

tools.name: Trivy v0.58 (fs mode)
tools.label: Trivy v0.58 (fs mode)
tools.appliesTo: All — npm, pip, Maven, Go, Cargo, RubyGems, NuGet
tools.changesWhen: New package ecosystems added; CVE severity thresholds change
tools.whenToUse: Default — one tool scans all package managers without extra config
tools.whenNot: Use Snyk when commercial fix PR automation or SBOM export is required
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Snyk: Trivy is free and open source; Snyk auto-creates fix PRs and has better remediation UX

tools.name: Snyk v1.1280
tools.label: Snyk v1.1280
tools.changesWhen: Snyk org policies change; ignored CVEs list changes
tools.whenToUse: When the team needs Snyk's automated fix PR and license scanning in one platform
tools.whenNot: Replace with Trivy when cost is a constraint
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Snyk auto-creates fix PRs; Trivy only reports findings — no auto-remediation

tools.name: OWASP Dependency-Check v10.0
tools.label: OWASP Dependency-Check v10.0
tools.changesWhen: NVD API key rotation; NVD data feed schema changes
tools.whenToUse: Java/Maven/Gradle repos where NVD-sourced CVE data is required by compliance policy
tools.whenNot: Skip when Trivy covers the language ecosystem and NVD-specific sourcing is not required
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Dependency-Check queries NVD directly; Trivy aggregates multiple advisory databases

tools.name: osv-scanner v1.8
tools.label: osv-scanner v1.8
tools.appliesTo: All — lockfile-based: npm, pip, Maven, Go, Cargo, RubyGems, NuGet, Composer, Gradle
tools.changesWhen: OSV database updated continuously; new lockfile formats added
tools.whenToUse: When Google OSV database coverage is required — broader advisory source than NVD alone; lockfile-based with zero false positives from missing lockfiles
tools.whenNot: Skip when Trivy fs mode is already configured and OSV-specific sourcing is not required by policy
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: osv-scanner uses the OSV database (superset of NVD for many ecosystems); Trivy aggregates multiple sources including OSV

tools.name: CodeQL v2.19
tools.label: CodeQL v2.19
tools.appliesTo: JS/TS, Python, Java, Go, C/C++, Ruby, Swift
tools.changesWhen: Language support matrix expands; query packs updated
tools.whenToUse: Default for GitHub repos on supported languages — deep dataflow and taint tracking
tools.whenNot: Use Semgrep for unsupported languages or rapid custom rule authoring
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Semgrep: CodeQL does deep semantic dataflow analysis; Semgrep has simpler rule syntax and faster scan time

tools.name: Semgrep v1.75
tools.label: Semgrep v1.75
tools.changesWhen: Custom ruleset updated; new language added to repo
tools.whenToUse: Languages not supported by CodeQL, or when custom security rules are needed in days not weeks
tools.whenNot: Skip when CodeQL coverage is sufficient for the languages in use
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs CodeQL: Semgrep rules are easier to write; CodeQL has deeper interprocedural analysis

tools.name: SonarCloud / SonarQube v10.5
tools.label: SonarCloud / SonarQube v10.5
tools.changesWhen: Quality gate rules updated; language analysis plugins updated
tools.output: SonarCloud PR decoration + JSON via API
tools.whenToUse: When code quality metrics (duplication, complexity, maintainability ratings) are needed alongside security findings
tools.whenNot: Skip when CodeQL coverage is sufficient and code quality gates are tracked elsewhere
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs CodeQL: SonarCloud covers code quality and security in one tool; CodeQL does deeper security dataflow analysis only

tools.name: FOSSA v3
tools.label: FOSSA v3
tools.changesWhen: Allowed license policy updated; new ecosystems added
tools.whenToUse: Commercial products where GPL or AGPL in a dependency creates legal risk — FOSSA blocks PRs automatically
tools.whenNot: Use ORT or licensefinder for internal tools with no strict compliance requirement
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs ORT: FOSSA enforces policy automatically and has commercial support; ORT is open source and self-hostable

tools.name: ORT — OSS Review Toolkit v25.0
tools.label: ORT — OSS Review Toolkit v25.0
tools.appliesTo: All — npm, Maven, pip, Cargo, Go modules, NuGet, RubyGems
tools.changesWhen: Policy rules updated; new package managers added to monorepo
tools.whenToUse: Monorepos with multiple package managers needing one open-source license audit tool
tools.whenNot: Use FOSSA when automated PR-blocking policy enforcement is required
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs FOSSA: ORT is fully open source and self-hostable; FOSSA has automated enforcement and commercial SLA

tools.name: licensefinder v7
tools.label: licensefinder v7
tools.changesWhen: Approved licenses list changes in doc/dependency_decisions.yml
tools.whenToUse: Internal tools needing a simple license audit without commercial tooling or ORT complexity
tools.whenNot: Replace with FOSSA or ORT when automated policy enforcement is needed
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs FOSSA: licensefinder is free and simple; FOSSA has automated policy enforcement and commercial support

tools.name: checkov v3
tools.label: checkov v3
tools.appliesTo: Terraform / Helm / K8s / CloudFormation / Dockerfile / ARM / Ansible
tools.changesWhen: New resource types added; policy framework updated

tools.name: KICS v2.1
tools.label: KICS v2.1

tools.label: Trivy v0.58 (config mode) — replaces tfsec

tools.whenNot: Skip when checkov or KICS is already configured. Note: tfsec was absorbed into this mode in 2023.

tools.name: Snyk IaC (part of Snyk CLI v1.1280+)
tools.label: Snyk IaC (part of Snyk CLI v1.1280+)
tools.tool: Snyk IaC (part of Snyk CLI v1.1280+)
tools.changesWhen: Snyk org IaC policies change

tools.whenNot: Skip when checkov is already configured — duplicate IaC scanning adds noise
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs checkov: Snyk IaC consolidates with Snyk SCA in one platform; checkov is open source with no commercial dependency

tools.name: gitleaks v8.21
tools.label: gitleaks v8.21
tools.changesWhen: Custom regex patterns added for org-specific secret formats
tools.whenToUse: Default — scans full repo history and PR diff for known secret patterns
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs truffleHog: gitleaks is faster with deterministic rules; truffleHog adds entropy detection for non-patterned secrets

tools.name: truffleHog v3.80
tools.label: truffleHog v3.80
tools.changesWhen: Entropy threshold tuned; custom detectors added
tools.whenToUse: When entropy-based detection is needed for non-patterned secrets that gitleaks misses
tools.whenNot: Skip when gitleaks rule coverage is sufficient
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs gitleaks: truffleHog finds secrets without fixed patterns; gitleaks is faster on known patterns

tools.name: GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) — Secret Scanning
tools.label: GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) — Secret Scanning

tools.name: Docker buildx v0.15

tools.whenNot: Use kaniko when Docker daemon is unavailable in the CI runner
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs kaniko: Docker buildx requires Docker daemon; kaniko runs rootless inside Kubernetes pods

tools.name: kaniko v1.23
tools.label: kaniko v1.23

tools.whenToUse: Kubernetes-native CI runners where Docker daemon is unavailable (Tekton, Argo Workflows)
tools.whenNot: Skip when Docker buildx is sufficient

tools.whenNot: Use cdxgen for Java/Maven/Gradle repos needing class-level dependency resolution

tools.output: Image pushed to registry with digest

tools.whenNot: Skip when Docker buildx or kaniko is already configured

tools.name: semantic-release v24
tools.label: semantic-release v24

tools.whenNot: Use Release Please when a review PR before tagging is required

tools.name: Release Please v4
tools.label: Release Please v4
tools.changesWhen: Release config changes; changelog sections changed
tools.output: Release PR + Git tag on merge of that PR
tools.whenToUse: When the team wants to review and edit release notes before the tag is cut
tools.whenNot: Skip when semantic-release's immediate tagging is acceptable

tools.name: Trivy v0.58 (image mode — registry pull)
tools.label: Trivy v0.58 (image mode — registry pull)
tools.changesWhen: CVE database auto-updates; severity threshold changes; new CVEs may appear after push
tools.ciIntegration: aquasecurity/trivy-action scanning the pushed registry digest
tools.whenToUse: Scans the pushed registry image — the actual artifact that will be signed. Never sign before scanning. Invariant I-P3-1.
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Phase 2 container scan: Phase 2 scans a local tarball; Phase 3 scans the registry image to catch base image updates that occurred after Phase 2 ran

tools.name: Grype v0.82
tools.label: Grype v0.82
tools.changesWhen: Grype DB auto-updates; Syft SBOM schema changes
tools.whenToUse: When Syft SBOM was generated in the same pipeline run and re-scanning from SBOM is faster than a full registry pull
tools.whenNot: Skip when Trivy is already configured
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Grype re-scans from SBOM (faster); Trivy scans the registry image directly every time

tools.name: Docker Scout
tools.label: Docker Scout
tools.changesWhen: Docker Scout policy rules updated
tools.output: SARIF / JSON + base image upgrade recommendation
tools.whenToUse: Docker Hub users wanting base image upgrade paths alongside CVE findings
tools.whenNot: Skip when Trivy or Grype is already configured
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Docker Scout recommends specific base image upgrades; Trivy reports CVEs without upgrade paths

tools.name: cosign v2.4 (keyless OIDC)
tools.label: cosign v2.4 (keyless OIDC)
tools.changesWhen: OIDC issuer changes; Rekor transparency log endpoint changes
tools.output: Sigstore signature stored in registry alongside image digest
tools.whenToUse: Always — Kyverno admission policy blocks unsigned images before pod start
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs notation (CNCF Notary): cosign uses Sigstore transparency log for public auditability; notation uses X.509 certificate chains

tools.name: Syft v1.6 + cosign v2.4 attest
tools.label: Syft v1.6 + cosign v2.4 attest
tools.changesWhen: SBOM format migration; attestation format updates; Rekor endpoint changes
tools.output: CycloneDX JSON SBOM attached as cosign attestation to the registry digest
tools.whenToUse: Always — SBOM attestation must be present before Registry stage. Invariant I-P3-2: SBOM after sign.
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs SPDX format: CycloneDX is NTIA-recommended and VEX-compatible; SPDX is better for license-focused analysis

tools.name: cdxgen v10.1 + cosign v2.4 attest
tools.label: cdxgen v10.1 + cosign v2.4 attest
tools.changesWhen: Java framework changes; cdxgen language plugin updates
tools.output: CycloneDX JSON SBOM (class-level) attested via cosign
tools.whenToUse: Java/Maven/Gradle repos where Syft misses class-level transitive JVM dependencies
tools.whenNot: Use Syft when multi-ecosystem scanning is needed
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Syft: cdxgen resolves JVM class-level dependencies; Syft is faster and covers more ecosystems

tools.name: slsa-github-generator v2.1
tools.label: slsa-github-generator v2.1
tools.appliesTo: Container images + binaries — GitHub Actions CI
tools.changesWhen: Generator major version update (breaking changes); Rekor endpoint changes
tools.output: SLSA Level 3 provenance JSON attested via cosign
tools.whenToUse: GitHub Actions CI — every build producing a promoted artifact. Invariant I-P3-4: SLSA after sign.
tools.whenNot: Use Tekton Chains when CI runs on Tekton Pipelines, not GitHub Actions
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Tekton Chains: slsa-github-generator works natively in GitHub Actions; Tekton Chains runs as a Kubernetes controller for Tekton CI

tools.name: Tekton Chains v0.22
tools.label: Tekton Chains v0.22
tools.changesWhen: Tekton Chains config changes; signing backend changes (Sigstore vs KMS)
tools.output: SLSA provenance stored as TaskRun annotation in the cluster
tools.ciIntegration: Installed as Kubernetes controller alongside Tekton Pipelines
tools.whenToUse: Kubernetes-native CI using Tekton Pipelines — Chains runs as a controller and signs every TaskRun automatically
tools.whenNot: Skip when using GitHub Actions — use slsa-github-generator instead
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs slsa-github-generator: Tekton Chains signs every TaskRun without workflow changes; slsa-github-generator requires a specific GitHub Actions workflow structure

tools.name: Language test runner (Jest / pytest / JUnit / go test) + Codecov
tools.label: Language test runner (Jest / pytest / JUnit / go test) + Codecov
tools.tool: Language test runner (Jest / pytest / JUnit / go test) + Codecov
tools.changesWhen: Test framework version change; coverage threshold tightened
tools.output: JUnit XML + Codecov JSON report + PR comment
tools.whenToUse: Every repo with application code — coverage gates block PRs below the threshold
tools.whenNot: Never skip for application repos
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Coveralls: Codecov has better GitHub PR comment integration and trend graphs; Coveralls is simpler but has fewer features

tools.name: OWASP ZAP v2.15
tools.label: OWASP ZAP v2.15
tools.changesWhen: ZAP ruleset updates; target URL or auth config changes
tools.ciIntegration: zaproxy/action-full-scan against the locally running container
tools.whenToUse: Web applications with exposed HTTP endpoints. Invariant I-P3-6: DAST after tests.
tools.whenNot: Skip for non-HTTP services: batch jobs, CLIs, queue processors, pure gRPC services
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Nuclei: ZAP does active spider-and-attack scanning; Nuclei runs template-based checks (faster, fewer false-positives)

tools.name: Nuclei v3.3
tools.label: Nuclei v3.3
tools.changesWhen: Template library updated (nuclei-templates repo); target URL changes
tools.whenToUse: API-heavy services where ZAP's spider-based approach produces excessive false-positives
tools.whenNot: Skip when OWASP ZAP full-scan coverage is sufficient for the web app surface
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs OWASP ZAP: Nuclei uses community templates (fast, low false-positive); ZAP does active spider-and-attack (thorough, more false-positives)

tools.name: k6 v0.53
tools.label: k6 v0.53
tools.changesWhen: Load profile changes; SLA thresholds tightened; new endpoints added
tools.whenToUse: Any HTTP service with a defined latency SLA — catches regressions before promotion
tools.whenNot: Skip for internal-only services with no latency SLA
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Artillery: k6 uses JavaScript scripts; Artillery uses YAML scenario definitions — pick based on team preference

tools.name: Artillery v2.0
tools.label: Artillery v2.0
tools.changesWhen: Load profile changes; WebSocket scenarios updated; target service changes
tools.whenToUse: Node.js teams preferring YAML-based scenario definitions, or services needing WebSocket load testing
tools.whenNot: Skip when k6 is already configured
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs k6: Artillery uses YAML scenarios (easier to read); k6 uses JavaScript (more flexible for complex logic)

tools.name: Gatling v3.11
tools.label: Gatling v3.11
tools.changesWhen: Simulation class changes; load injection profile changes
tools.whenToUse: JVM-heavy teams (Java/Kotlin/Scala) where Gatling's Scala DSL is the team standard
tools.whenNot: Skip when k6 is already configured and team is not JVM-based
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs k6: Gatling uses Scala/Java DSL and has richer HTML reports; k6 uses JavaScript and is simpler to set up

tools.name: slackapi/slack-github-action v2
tools.label: slackapi/slack-github-action v2
tools.changesWhen: Slack channel changes; bot token rotation
tools.whenToUse: Any team using Slack — notifies build success, failure, and image digest on every main build
tools.whenNot: Replace with PagerDuty when build failures require on-call escalation
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs PagerDuty: Slack sends to a shared channel; PagerDuty pages the on-call engineer directly

tools.name: PagerDuty Events API v2
tools.label: PagerDuty Events API v2
tools.changesWhen: PagerDuty service routing key changes; escalation policy changes
tools.whenToUse: When Phase 3 build failures require immediate on-call escalation outside business hours
tools.whenNot: Use Slack for notifications that do not require immediate human response
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Slack: PagerDuty escalates to the on-call engineer; Slack sends to a channel that may not be monitored off-hours

tools.name: Microsoft Teams incoming webhook
tools.label: Microsoft Teams incoming webhook
tools.changesWhen: Teams channel webhook URL rotates; Teams tenant migration
tools.ciIntegration: aliencube/microsoft-teams-deploy-card or curl to webhook URL
tools.whenToUse: Organizations where Microsoft Teams is the primary communication tool
tools.whenNot: Skip when Slack is already configured
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Slack: Teams webhook is free with M365; Slack requires a separate Slack workspace account

tools.name: GHCR — GitHub Container Registry
tools.label: GHCR — GitHub Container Registry
tools.changesWhen: Org transfer; retention policy changes
tools.whenToUse: Default for GitHub repos — zero extra cost, tight OIDC integration
tools.whenNot: Use ECR when AWS VPC endpoint access is required
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs ECR: GHCR needs no AWS account; ECR has deeper AWS IAM and lifecycle policy control

tools.name: ECR — Elastic Container Registry
tools.label: ECR — Elastic Container Registry
tools.changesWhen: AWS account migration; lifecycle policy changes
tools.whenToUse: AWS workloads requiring VPC endpoint registry access or cross-account pull policies
tools.whenNot: Skip when not on AWS
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: ECR has deeper AWS IAM integration; GHCR needs no AWS account

tools.name: Google Artifact Registry
tools.label: Google Artifact Registry
tools.license: Google Cloud — pay per GB stored + transferred
tools.changesWhen: GCP project migration; service account or Workload Identity changes; repository format changes
tools.whenToUse: GCP workloads requiring Artifact Analysis (CVE scanning), VPC Service Controls, or Binary Authorization integration
tools.whenNot: Skip when not on GCP
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: Artifact Registry integrates with GCP Binary Authorization and Artifact Analysis natively; GHCR needs no GCP account

tools.name: Harbor v2.11
tools.label: Harbor v2.11
tools.appliesTo: All container workloads — on-premises or private cloud
tools.changesWhen: Harbor admin policies change; replication rules update; built-in Trivy integration config changes
tools.output: Immutable image digest + built-in scan results
tools.ciIntegration: Docker login + push to Harbor domain
tools.whenToUse: On-premises or air-gapped environments where cloud registries are prohibited by security policy
tools.whenNot: Use GHCR or ECR when cloud-hosted registry is acceptable
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: Harbor is fully self-hosted with built-in Trivy scanning and replication; GHCR needs no infrastructure management

tools.name: Docker Hub
tools.label: Docker Hub
tools.license: Commercial — free tier for public images; paid for private
tools.changesWhen: Docker Hub org policies change; rate limit policy changes
tools.output: Image digest in public or private registry
tools.whenToUse: Public open-source images that need maximum pull accessibility across all cloud providers and tools
tools.whenNot: Use GHCR for private repos — Docker Hub's free tier rate-limits unauthenticated pulls
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: Docker Hub has maximum public reach; GHCR is free for private GitHub repos with no rate limits

tools.name: cosign v2.4 verify
tools.label: cosign v2.4 verify
tools.changesWhen: OIDC issuer changes; expected workflow ref changes
tools.output: CLI pass/fail — exits non-zero if signature is invalid or missing
tools.whenToUse: Re-verify after push to registry — confirms the pushed digest matches the signed digest before any promotion
tools.whenNot: Never skip — Kyverno also blocks unsigned images at admission but only when a pod is submitted
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Kyverno admission check alone: cosign verify is an explicit pre-promotion check; Kyverno blocks at deployment but does not block registry storage

tools.name: cosign v2.4 attach sbom
tools.label: cosign v2.4 attach sbom
tools.changesWhen: SBOM format migration (CycloneDX → SPDX)
tools.output: SBOM stored as OCI artifact attached to the registry digest
tools.whenToUse: Always — SBOM must be retrievable from the registry digest before any consumer or promotion can use it
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs oras CLI: cosign stores SBOM as a Sigstore attestation with provenance; oras stores as an arbitrary OCI artifact without signing

tools.name: slsa-verifier v2.5 + cosign v2.4
tools.label: slsa-verifier v2.5 + cosign v2.4
tools.changesWhen: Generator version updates; Rekor endpoint changes
tools.output: SLSA provenance attached as OCI artifact + verification pass/fail
tools.whenToUse: Always — SLSA provenance must be attached and verified before any promotion starts. SLSA Level 3 requires this.
tools.whenNot: Never skip
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs in-toto verification: slsa-verifier is SLSA-native with no custom layout; in-toto requires a custom supply chain layout file

tools.name: GitHub Actions + yq v4 + peter-evans/create-pull-request
tools.label: GitHub Actions + yq v4 + peter-evans/create-pull-request
tools.changesWhen: GitOps repo structure changes; image digest field path changes in overlays
tools.output: Pull request bumping the image digest in the target environment overlay
tools.whenToUse: Every promotion — the PR is the explicit promotion gate requiring human approval before merge
tools.whenNot: Never use direct push to the GitOps repo — that bypasses the review gate
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Flux image automation: manual PR gives an explicit review gate; Flux auto-PR removes the human approval step for fully automatic promotion

tools.name: ArgoCD v2.12
tools.label: ArgoCD v2.12
tools.changesWhen: ArgoCD Application config changes; sync policy changes; RBAC policy changes
tools.output: Kubernetes resources reconciled — sync status: Synced / OutOfSync / Degraded
tools.whenToUse: Default GitOps controller — sync Kubernetes manifests from Git with UI visibility and multi-cluster support
tools.whenNot: Use Flux v2 when a UI-free, purely Kubernetes-native controller is required
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Flux v2: ArgoCD has a UI dashboard and richer RBAC model; Flux is purely API-driven with no UI dependency

tools.name: Flux v2.4
tools.label: Flux v2.4
tools.changesWhen: Flux source / Kustomization CRD changes; image policy changes
tools.output: Kubernetes resources reconciled via Flux CRDs
tools.whenToUse: When Kubernetes-native, UI-free GitOps is required — Flux runs as Kubernetes controllers only
tools.whenNot: Skip when ArgoCD is already deployed
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs ArgoCD: Flux has no UI and is fully CRD-driven; ArgoCD has a richer UI and centralized multi-cluster view

tools.name: cosign v2.4 verify
tools.label: cosign v2.4 verify
tools.changesWhen: OIDC issuer changes; expected workflow ref changes
tools.output: CLI pass/fail — blocks promotion if signature is invalid or missing
tools.whenToUse: Every promotion in every environment — re-verify before deploying to that environment
tools.whenNot: Never skip — this is the only explicit check between Registry and cluster admission
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs relying on Kyverno alone: cosign verify fails the pipeline before deployment; Kyverno only blocks after a pod is submitted to the API server

tools.name: pytest / Newman (Postman CLI) / k6 — per environment type
tools.label: pytest / Newman (Postman CLI) / k6 — per environment type
tools.tool: pytest / Newman (Postman CLI) / k6 — per environment type
tools.changesWhen: Test suite changes; environment-specific endpoint or auth changes
tools.whenToUse: DEV: integration tests. TEST: integration + contract tests. STAGING: regression tests.
tools.whenNot: Replace with Argo Rollouts for PROD — live traffic canary analysis replaces pre-deployment test suites
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs smoke tests only: full env tests catch integration failures; smoke tests only verify the app started and responds

tools.name: GitHub Environments with protection rules
tools.label: GitHub Environments with protection rules
tools.changesWhen: Required reviewers change; wait timer changes; deployment branch policy changes
tools.output: N/A — workflow pauses until explicitly approved
tools.whenToUse: DEV · TEST · STAGING — explicit human approval required before each promotion
tools.whenNot: Replace with Argo Rollouts + Monitor for PROD
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs automatic promotion on test pass: Gate requires explicit approval; auto-promotion promotes on green tests without human sign-off

tools.name: Argo Rollouts v1.8
tools.label: Argo Rollouts v1.8
tools.stage: Argo Rollouts — PROD only (replaces Env tests)
tools.changesWhen: Canary step percentages change; traffic split policy changes
tools.output: Rollout status: progressing / paused / degraded / healthy
tools.ciIntegration: kubectl argo rollouts plugin + Argo Rollouts controller
tools.whenToUse: PROD only — shifts real traffic gradually to the new version instead of big-bang deploy
tools.whenNot: Skip for DEV / TEST / STAGING — use Gate. Use Flagger when Flux is the GitOps controller.
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs Flagger: Argo Rollouts is native to ArgoCD; Flagger is native to Flux — match to the GitOps controller in use

tools.name: Argo Rollouts AnalysisTemplate + Prometheus v3.0
tools.label: Argo Rollouts AnalysisTemplate + Prometheus v3.0
tools.changesWhen: SLO thresholds change; Prometheus query changes; metric names change after instrumentation updates
tools.output: Rollout auto-complete or auto-rollback based on live metric analysis
tools.ciIntegration: Argo Rollouts AnalysisTemplate CRD — no separate CI action needed
tools.whenToUse: PROD only — auto-completes or auto-rolls back based on error rate, latency, and custom SLO metrics
tools.whenNot: Skip for DEV / TEST / STAGING — Gate handles those environments
tools.primaryTradeoff: vs manual monitoring during deploy: automated analysis removes human watch-and-decide; manual monitoring requires someone watching dashboards during the canary window

buildAxes.name: AUTH
buildAxes.label: Authentication method

valueNotes.none: No authentication — all requests pass through
valueNotes.jwt: JWT Bearer token validation middleware on all routes. Invalid or missing token → 401.
valueNotes.oauth2: OAuth2 client credentials flow — machine-to-machine API access
valueNotes.apikey: API key header validation — checks X-API-Key header on every request

buildAxes.scope: Scope: applies to 20 of the 34 Tier 1 services — all server services with a request entry point

valueNotes.node: Default. Used for all TypeScript/Express/Fastify backends.
valueNotes.python: Used for FastAPI, Django, Flask services.
valueNotes.golang: Builder stage compiles binary. Runtime is distroless — no shell, minimal attack surface.
valueNotes.java: Builds JAR in builder, runs JRE-only in runtime.
valueNotes.dotnet: Publishes self-contained binary. Runtime is ASP.NET Core image.
valueNotes.rust: Compiles release binary. Runtime is bare Alpine — binary is statically linked.
valueNotes.elixir: Builds Mix release. Runtime runs the release tarball on bare Alpine.
valueNotes.ruby: Used for Rails API services. Puma runs in runtime stage.
valueNotes.php: PHP-FPM. Compose installs vendor in builder. Runtime runs FPM.

buildAxes.name: BUILD_TOOL
buildAxes.label: TypeScript compiler / bundler

valueNotes.tsc: Default. Best type checking. Required for libraries that export types.
valueNotes.esbuild: When build speed matters. Lambda deployments. Does not type-check.
valueNotes.swc: When tsc is too slow but per-file output is needed. Does not type-check.

buildAxes.scope: Scope: applies to 13 of the 34 Tier 1 services

buildAxes.name: COMPLIANCE
buildAxes.label: Compliance preset

valueNotes.hipaa: PHI field masking in all log output, audit trail middleware, verbose error messages disabled
valueNotes.pci: TLS enforced on all routes, PCI audit headers added, debug endpoints removed, plaintext-secrets startup check
valueNotes.pipeda: Consent check middleware, data residency header (X-Data-Residency: CA), PII masking in logs
valueNotes.fips: FIPS-140-2 OpenSSL module active, non-FIPS cipher suites disabled
valueNotes.soc2: Immutable audit log, access control middleware, debug mode disabled
valueNotes.cmmc: Full audit log on every request, debug routes removed, no outbound internet at runtime, CUI data handling
valueNotes.nerc: Network isolation config, no internet egress, OT/IT boundary enforcement headers

buildAxes.scope: Scope: applies to 20 of the 34 Tier 1 services — all server services that process HTTP requests

buildAxes.name: OBSERVABILITY
buildAxes.label: Observability stack

valueNotes.otel: OpenTelemetry SDK + OTLP exporter for traces, metrics, and logs
valueNotes.datadog: Datadog tracer + APM agent configured at service startup

buildAxes.scope: Scope: applies to 20 of the 34 Tier 1 services — all server services with HTTP request handlers

buildAxes.name: ORM
buildAxes.label: Database access layer
buildAxes.scope: Scope: applies to 14 of the 34 Tier 1 services — pure API server services only (groups 14–23)

buildAxes.name: PKG_MGR
buildAxes.label: Package manager

valueNotes.npm: Default. ci enforces lockfile — no version drift in builds.
valueNotes.pnpm: Faster installs, hard-linked node_modules. Requires corepack enable in Dockerfile.
valueNotes.yarn: Yarn Classic (v1). Yarn Berry (v2+) not supported — PnP mode breaks the build.
valueNotes.bun: Fastest installs. Use only if production runtime also uses Bun — bun:latest image required.
valueNotes.pip: Default for Python. Simple and universal.
valueNotes.poetry: Strict dependency resolution. Requires pyproject.toml present.
valueNotes.uv: Rust-based pip replacement. 10-100x faster installs. Uses same requirements.txt format.

buildAxes.scope: Scope: applies to 14 of the 34 Tier 1 services

valueNotes.alpine: Default for all services. Smallest attack surface with shell access.
valueNotes.slim: When a package requires glibc (native modules, some ML libs). Alpine uses musl.
valueNotes.fips: Required when COMPLIANCE=fips . US federal workloads. NIST 140-2 cryptography.

images.name: scratch
images.label: scratch
images.whenToUse: Statically-linked Go (CGO_ENABLED=0) or Rust binaries. Zero attack surface required. No CVEs possible — no packages.
images.whenNot: Any binary that requires libc, shell, dynamic linking, or CA certificates (use Distroless static instead).
images.primaryTradeoff: Zero attack surface, zero image size vs zero tooling — no shell, no certs, no debug tools. Distroless static adds ~2MB but includes CA certs.

images.name: Alpine Linux 3.21
images.label: Alpine Linux 3.21
images.whenToUse: Smallest possible image where no musl-incompatible binary is in the stack. Go (CGO disabled), Python (pure-Python packages), static Rust.
images.whenNot: Any binary compiled against glibc — Python native extensions (cryptography, lxml, numpy), Node.js native modules (bcrypt, sharp), or cgo-enabled Go.
images.primaryTradeoff: Smallest image (~7MB vs Debian-slim ~75MB) vs musl libc breaks ~15% of precompiled binaries without rebuild.

images.name: UBI-micro 9
images.label: UBI-micro 9
images.whenToUse: FIPS required. Red Hat / OpenShift environment. No runtime package installation needed — all packages layered at build time in Dockerfile.
images.whenNot: Need to install packages at container runtime. Need a shell in the final image. Non-Red Hat environment.
images.primaryTradeoff: Smallest RHEL-based FIPS image (~27MB) vs no package manager — must build all layers in the Dockerfile, cannot dnf install at runtime.

images.name: UBI-minimal 9
images.label: UBI-minimal 9
images.whenToUse: FIPS required. Need to install a small number of packages. Red Hat ecosystem.
images.whenNot: Need full dnf (use UBI-standard). Pure build-time layering is sufficient (use UBI-micro and save 10MB).
images.primaryTradeoff: FIPS + microdnf package installer vs 10MB larger than UBI-micro. Full dnf is not available — use UBI-standard for that.

images.name: Distroless (gcr.io)
images.label: Distroless (gcr.io)
images.whenToUse: glibc binary compat required. No shell in the final image. Go, Java, Python, Node.js apps where language-specific variant is available.
images.whenNot: FIPS required (use UBI). Need a shell in the final image. RHEL/OpenShift environment.
images.primaryTradeoff: glibc compat + no shell vs no FIPS, no official CIS benchmark, Debian lifecycle dependency.

images.name: Wolfi / Chainguard
images.label: Wolfi / Chainguard
images.fromTag: cgr.dev/chainguard/wolfi-base:latestNote: Wolfi's :latest is a rolling release — pin to a date-stamped digest for reproducible builds (e.g. cgr.dev/chainguard/wolfi-base:latest@sha256:...).
images.whenToUse: SBOM-native required. Supply chain compliance (SLSA Level 3, EO 14028). glibc needed but Alpine-sized image wanted.
images.whenNot: Free tier only with strict budget. Red Hat / OpenShift ecosystem. DISA STIG required.
images.primaryTradeoff: SBOM-native + glibc + Alpine-sized image vs newer ecosystem with fewer third-party packages, FIPS requires paid Chainguard subscription.

images.name: Azure Linux 3 (CBL-Mariner)
images.label: Azure Linux 3 (CBL-Mariner)
images.whenToUse: Azure-native workloads. AKS clusters. FIPS on Azure Government. Microsoft enterprise support required.
images.whenNot: Non-Azure environments. RHEL ecosystem required. DISA STIG officially required.
images.primaryTradeoff: FIPS + Azure-native + tdnf package manager vs Microsoft-locked, small community, 2-year release cycle.

images.name: Debian 12 (Bookworm)
images.label: Debian 12 (Bookworm)
images.subtitle: stable / slim
images.whenToUse: Maximum package availability. glibc binary compat for every pre-compiled binary. Base image for Ubuntu, Kali, Mint. No FIPS required.
images.whenNot: FIPS required. Enterprise vendor support SLA required. RHEL ecosystem expected.
images.primaryTradeoff: Largest package ecosystem + free vs no FIPS, no vendor support SLA, no DISA STIG.

images.name: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
images.label: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
images.subtitle: (Jammy Jellyfish)
images.whenToUse: Most common dev environment. FIPS needed with Canonical support (Ubuntu Pro — FIPS 140-2 module). DISA STIG required. 22.04 still needed when 24.04 toolchain compatibility is uncertain.
images.whenNot: RHEL ecosystem required. RPM-native tooling. Budget constraint (FIPS requires Ubuntu Pro paid tier).
images.primaryTradeoff: Most common OS (lowest developer friction) vs FIPS requires Ubuntu Pro subscription, DISA STIG tooling requires Pro.

images.name: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS
images.label: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS
images.subtitle: (Noble Numbat)
images.whenToUse: Default GitHub Actions runner (ubuntu-latest since Jan 2025). New projects. Longest support window (2036 with Legacy Support). Latest toolchain (Python 3.12, GCC 13, kernel 6.8). DISA STIG published 2025.
images.whenNot: FIPS independently certified required — 24.04 FIPS 140-3 module is in validation progress via Ubuntu Pro; use 22.04 if a current FIPS 140-2 certificate number is required by the auditor.
images.primaryTradeoff: Longest support + default CI runner + DISA STIG vs FIPS 140-3 certification still completing (use 22.04 for FIPS-required environments until 24.04 cert is finalized).

images.name: Fedora 42
images.label: Fedora 42
images.whenToUse: Testing new RHEL features before they land in RHEL. Cutting-edge package versions. Developer workstations.
images.whenNot: Production container base (13-month EOL). FIPS independently certified required. DISA STIG or CIS required. Long-running servers.
images.primaryTradeoff: Latest RHEL-bound features + free vs 13-month lifecycle, not production-safe for servers or containers.

images.name: Amazon Linux 2023
images.label: Amazon Linux 2023
images.useContext: Container base · EC2 · Lambda · CI runner
images.whenToUse: AWS-native workloads. Lambda container images. EC2 with FIPS in GovCloud. AWS manages kernel patches for Nitro hypervisor.
images.whenNot: Non-AWS environments. RHEL ecosystem migration. OpenShift. On-premises bare-metal.
images.primaryTradeoff: AWS-optimized kernel + FIPS + free on EC2 vs AWS-locked, packages lag RHEL by one cycle, no DISA STIG officially.

images.name: VMware Photon OS 5
images.label: VMware Photon OS 5
images.whenToUse: VMware vSphere / Tanzu / Broadcom ecosystem. vSphere-native container workloads. FIPS-capable environment on VMware (Photon 5 FIPS validation in progress).
images.whenNot: FIPS certificate number required by auditor — Photon 5 FIPS validation not yet independently certified (Photon 3.0 had the last certified module). Broadcom licensing uncertainty post-acquisition. No CIS benchmark.
images.primaryTradeoff: VMware-optimized vs FIPS not independently certified for Photon 5 (Photon 3.0 module held the certificate), Broadcom acquisition, small community.

images.name: RHEL 9
images.label: RHEL 9
images.whenToUse: FedRAMP High, DoD IL4+, financial sector. FIPS + DISA STIG both required. Red Hat subscription available. ISV software with RHEL certification.
images.whenNot: No Red Hat subscription budget (self-support from $349/year/server, Standard ~$800, Premium ~$1,300). Cost-sensitive non-regulated workload. Non-enterprise use.
images.primaryTradeoff: Best compliance coverage (FIPS + STIG + CIS all official) vs requires paid Red Hat subscription.

images.name: CentOS Stream 9
images.label: CentOS Stream 9
images.whenToUse: Testing RHEL 9 features pre-release. No subscription cost. Dev/staging environments where pre-release patches are acceptable.
images.whenNot: Production. FIPS certification required. DISA STIG required. Any regulated environment.
images.primaryTradeoff: Free + RHEL-compatible package format vs receives changes before RHEL QA, not production-safe.

images.name: Rocky Linux 9
images.label: Rocky Linux 9
images.whenToUse: RHEL-compatible, no subscription cost. Community support acceptable. Non-DoD/non-FedRAMP environments.
images.whenNot: Independent FIPS certificate required. DISA STIG officially required. Red Hat support SLA required. ISV RHEL certification required.
images.primaryTradeoff: Free RHEL-compatible vs FIPS not independently certified (uses RHEL crypto code without its own certificate number), community support only.

images.name: AlmaLinux 9
images.label: AlmaLinux 9
images.whenToUse: CentOS 7/8 migration path. RHEL-compatible. CloudLinux commercial backing. Community support acceptable.
images.whenNot: Independent FIPS certificate required. Red Hat support SLA required. ISV RHEL certification required.
images.primaryTradeoff: Free + CentOS migration tooling + CloudLinux backing vs same FIPS caveat as Rocky (no independent certificate).

images.name: Oracle Linux 9
images.label: Oracle Linux 9
images.eolSupport: Until RHEL 9 EOL (2032) + Oracle Premier Support
images.whenToUse: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). Oracle Database on Linux. FIPS independently certified required. DISA STIG required (OL9 STIG published 2024). Oracle enterprise support required.
images.whenNot: Non-Oracle environments. AWS/Azure native. No Oracle licensing budget. Red Hat ISV certification required (Oracle clone does not carry Red Hat cert).
images.primaryTradeoff: Independently FIPS-certified + official DISA STIG + Oracle support vs Oracle licensing costs for Premier Support, OCI-centric kernel optimizations.

images.name: Flatcar Container Linux
images.label: Flatcar Container Linux
images.whenToUse: Kubernetes node OS. Immutable OS required. Atomic OS updates with auto-rollback. Azure / AKS workloads. SSH still available (vs Talos).
images.whenNot: Non-Kubernetes workloads. Need to install host-level packages. FIPS required. DISA STIG required.
images.primaryTradeoff: Immutable + auto-update + SSH available vs no FIPS, no DISA STIG, Microsoft-controlled release cadence.

images.name: Talos Linux 1.10
images.label: Talos Linux 1.10
images.whenToUse: Kubernetes-only clusters. Maximum attack surface reduction — no SSH, no shell, no unnecessary processes. GitOps-driven cluster management via talosctl API.
images.whenNot: Any non-Kubernetes workload. Need SSH access to nodes. Need custom kernel modules. FIPS certificate independently required. DISA STIG required.
images.primaryTradeoff: Smallest attack surface of any K8s node OS (no shell, no SSH) vs absolutely no host-level interactive access, ~1-year release lifecycle.

images.name: Bottlerocket 1.x
images.label: Bottlerocket 1.x
images.whenToUse: EKS on AWS. FIPS in GovCloud. Auto-patching required. Minimal attack surface on K8s nodes with AWS management integration (SSM).
images.whenNot: Non-EKS Kubernetes. On-premises. DISA STIG officially required. Non-AWS environments.
images.primaryTradeoff: FIPS + immutable + AWS-managed patching vs AWS-locked, no DISA STIG, no SSH (admin container required for debug).

images.name: Google COS
images.label: Google COS
images.minImage: ~2.5 GB (GKE node OS — not a Dockerfile FROM image)
images.whenToUse: GKE node OS. Automatic security patching required. No node-level customization needed.
images.whenNot: Non-GKE Kubernetes. Need custom kernel modules. Need host-level packages. DISA STIG required.
images.primaryTradeoff: Auto-patched + container-optimized kernel vs immutable (cannot apt-get on the node), 1-year release cycle, GKE-locked.

compliance.name: cmmc
compliance.label: CMMC — Defense supply chain
compliance.middleware: Audit log (cmmc_audit), debug→404, NO_EGRESS=true, verbose errors disabled

compliance.name: fips
compliance.label: FIPS 140-2 — US federal cryptography

distroSupport.why: FIPS requires validated OpenSSL (FIPS object module). Alpine uses musl libc — no FIPS-validated crypto. RHEL, Oracle Linux 9, and Amazon Linux 2023 ship OpenSSL 3.x with FIPS validation certificates. CentOS Stream 9 has no independent FIPS certificate.

requiredControls.startup: OpenSSL FIPS not active → throws / Fatal

compliance.useWhen: US federal / defense. All crypto must be FIPS-validated.

compliance.name: hipaa
compliance.label: HIPAA — Healthcare data

distroSupport.why: HIPAA requires audit logging (auditd) and access controls. Alpine lacks auditd in base. Debian/Ubuntu ship auditd and have long LTS cycles for patch compliance evidence.

requiredControls.auditLog: hipaa_access · actor, action, resource, request-id, timestamp
requiredControls.piiMasking: ssn, dob, mrn, diagnosis, patientName, address, phone → ***REDACTED***

compliance.middleware: Audit log (every request) + PHI masking in logger + verbose errors disabled
compliance.useWhen: US healthcare. Service handles PHI (patient data).

compliance.name: nerc
compliance.label: NERC CIP — Energy grid / OT-IT boundary
compliance.useWhen: Electric utilities. Service operates in OT/IT boundary.

compliance.name: pci
compliance.label: PCI DSS — Payment card data

distroSupport.why: PCI DSS v4.0 requires removing all unnecessary software components. Distroless and Wolfi have near-zero package footprint — easier to pass PCI 6.3.3 (no unnecessary software).

compliance.useWhen: Cardholder data environment. Service processes card data.

compliance.name: pipeda
compliance.label: PIPEDA — Canadian personal information

distroSupport.why: PIPEDA is primarily a data-handling regulation, not a cryptography standard. Any distro with current security patches satisfies the technical baseline. Debian/Ubuntu preferred for long LTS support windows matching data retention periods.

compliance.middleware: Consent gate (POST/PUT/PATCH), X-Data-Residency: CA header, PII masking
compliance.useWhen: Canadian orgs. Service collects personal information.

compliance.name: soc2
compliance.label: SOC 2 — Service organization controls
compliance.middleware: Immutable audit log (soc2_audit event) + verbose errors disabled
compliance.useWhen: SaaS. Service stores customer data. SOC 2 audit required.

compliance.name: standard
compliance.label: standard — No extra middleware
compliance.useWhen: Default. No regulated data. No compliance requirement.

industries.name: Retail Banking
industries.frameworkNotes: OSFI B-13 requires vendor notification for cloud. Core system data must reside in Canada unless OSFI-approved.

employersByProvince.Ontario: RBC, TD Bank, Scotiabank, BMO, CIBC, Equitable Bank (Toronto)

entry: No security clearance required; standard criminal background check
AWS/Azure Certified Solutions Architect valued by Big 5
CISSP or CISM for security-adjacent roles
French-English bilingual valued at Desjardins, National Bank, Laurentian
Entry difficulty 4 = interview bar is high (system design), not role scarcity

industries.name: Investment Banking
industries.frameworkNotes: CIRO Rule 3200 data segregation. MNPI firewall enforcement between trading desks. Trading systems require sub-50ms latency.

keySecurityRequirements: audit trail for all trades and communications

industries.name: Life Insurance
industries.frameworkNotes: Health data triggers PHIPA (ON) or provincial equivalent. OSFI Guideline E-21 for operational risk. Federal insurers under OSFI; provincial under provincial acts.

industries.name: P&C Insurance
industries.frameworkNotes: Auto insurance publicly administered in BC (ICBC), MB (MPI), SK (SGI), QC (SAAQ) — separate regulatory regime. IFRS 17 financial reporting compliance.

industries.name: Asset Management
industries.frameworkNotes: NI 81-102 governs mutual funds. ETFs under NI 41-101. NI 31-103 registration required. Fund accounting systems require 99.9% uptime SLA.

industries.name: Fintech & Neobanks
industries.frameworkNotes: Canada Open Banking framework: federal progress disrupted by April 2025 election; legislative timeline under review. OSFI B-13 applies to tech-heavy operations. FINTRAC MSB registration mandatory for money services businesses.

industries.name: Credit Unions
industries.frameworkNotes: Provincially regulated only (no federal charter). Desjardins Group (QC) is Canada's largest credit union network. Deposit insurance varies by province.

industries.name: Payments Processing
industries.frameworkNotes: Payments Canada Modernization: Lynx RTGS system live, RTR real-time rail programme restructured (2024) — launch timeline under review. Interac dominant domestic network. ISO 20022 migration underway.

industries.name: Hospitals & Health Systems
industries.frameworkNotes: Data must stay within the province for patient records. PHIPA (ON) most stringent provincial law. Federal hospitals (Veterans Affairs, DND) follow PIPEDA additionally.

keySecurityRequirements: PHI encryption at rest and in transit
access controls per circle of care

industries.name: Provincial Health Insurance
mandatoryCompliance: Provincial health insurance acts (OHIP-ON, MSP-BC, AHCIP-AB)
industries.frameworkNotes: Each province operates own health insurance program. Data residency within province mandatory. Federal transfers via Canada Health Act. Billing systems require HL7/FHIR integration security.

industries.name: Pharmaceutical
industries.frameworkNotes: Health Canada requires 21 CFR Part 11-equivalent electronic records validation. CDSA for controlled substance tracking. GCP/GMP validation requirements affect CI/CD pipeline (change control procedures).

keySecurityRequirements: chain of custody for controlled substances

industries.name: Biotech & Life Sciences Research
industries.frameworkNotes: Genomic data requires explicit consent under TCPS 2 Article 13. ELSI (Ethical, Legal, Social Implications) framework applies. National Security Guidelines for Research Partnerships (2023) affect foreign-funded research.

keySecurityRequirements: research data sovereignty (data stays in Canada)

industries.name: Medical Devices
industries.frameworkNotes: Health Canada Class I–IV determines regulatory pathway. Class III/IV require QMS. SaMD (Software as Medical Device) follows IMDRF guidance. SBOM required for Class III/IV devices.

auditRequirements: Design history file: product lifetime + 10 yr

industries.name: Clinical Diagnostics & Labs
industries.frameworkNotes: LIS (Laboratory Information System) integration requires HL7 v2/FHIR security. Result sign-off workflow integrity mandatory. POCT (Point-of-Care Testing) devices follow specific Health Canada regulations.

keySecurityRequirements: access controls by ordering physician circle of care

industries.name: Telehealth & Digital Health
regulators: Provincial colleges of regulated health professionals
industries.frameworkNotes: Canadian data centres required for patient video/audio. Provincial practitioner licensing restricts cross-provincial care delivery. CASL applies to appointment reminders.

industries.name: Long-term Care & Home Care
regulators: Provincial ministries of health and long-term care
industries.frameworkNotes: IoT/smart home integration for resident monitoring requires additional security controls. Mobile care worker apps require MDM. Data sharing with acute care follows provincial health information custodian rules.

industries.name: Federal Government
industries.frameworkNotes: GC PBMM mandatory for cloud workloads. CCCS ITSG-33 IT security controls. Data residency in Canada mandatory. PSPC cloud procurement rules apply. ATO (Authority to Operate) process required.

keySecurityRequirements: identity via GCKey or Sign-In Canada

employersByProvince.British Columbia: CRA Pacific, DFO, CBSA Pacific, Pacific Region digital (Victoria/Vancouver)
employersByProvince.Quebec: CBC/Radio-Canada tech, NRC, CFIA, IRB Quebec (Montréal/Quebec City)
employersByProvince.Alberta: Service Canada Alberta, CBSA Prairie Region (Edmonton)

Secret clearance required for approximately 40% of federal IT positions
Top Secret required for DND, CSE, and CSIS-adjacent roles
Canadian citizenship required for Secret clearance and above
Permanent Residents eligible for Reliability Status only
GC Cloud experience (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government) increasingly valued

industries.name: Provincial Government
industries.frameworkNotes: Data residency within province mandatory for most provincial systems. BC Cloud Hosting Services, Ontario Government cloud policy vary. Identity via provincial SSO.

keySecurityRequirements: Access controls per provincial RBAC policies

employersByProvince.Ontario: Ontario Digital Service, ServiceOntario, MGCS, OPS tech (Toronto/Ottawa)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: BC Digital Service (Victoria), OCIO BC, Ministry of Health IT
employersByProvince.Alberta: Service Alberta, Alberta Digital, ASIRT tech (Edmonton)
employersByProvince.New Brunswick: Service New Brunswick (Fredericton — shared services for Atlantic provinces)

No clearance required for some contracted roles through provincial tech procurement
Entry difficulty 3 = stable hiring; BC Digital is particularly active
Public sector pay grids cap salaries below private sector; compensated by stability
Service New Brunswick is a shared services model hiring for multiple Atlantic provinces

industries.name: Municipal Government
industries.frameworkNotes: Smart city deployments require Privacy Impact Assessments (PIAs). IoT/sensor data in public spaces regulated by provincial FIPPA. Procurement through municipal procurement offices.

keySecurityRequirements: open data privacy review before publication

industries.name: Crown Corporations
industries.frameworkNotes: Each Crown operates under its own mandate act (CBC Act, Canada Post Act, VIA Rail). Commercial activities follow PIPEDA; government activities follow Privacy Act. Canada Post is critical infrastructure designated.

employersByProvince.Ontario: Canada Post HQ, CBC English digital, EDC, BDC, Via Rail IT (Ottawa/Toronto)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: Canada Post Pacific, NAV CANADA Pacific (Vancouver)
employersByProvince.Alberta: NAV CANADA Prairie, Canada Post Prairie ops (Calgary)

entry: Reliability Status clearance typically required
Entry difficulty 3 = moderate; stable hiring across Crown Corporations
Canada Post is a large tech employer — 500+ IT staff in Ottawa HQ
CBC/Radio-Canada requires bilingual candidates for all Montréal-based roles
NAV CANADA roles require Transport Canada security clearance

industries.name: Public Safety & Law Enforcement
industries.frameworkNotes: RCMP CPIC system requires CCCS approval. Provincial police systems (OPP, SQ) follow provincial data governance. Biometric data (fingerprints, DNA) under Privacy Act with additional restrictions.

keySecurityRequirements: intercept capability per Criminal Code Part VI

industries.name: Justice & Courts
industries.frameworkNotes: Electronic court filing systems (eCourt, Caselines) require strict access controls. Publication bans enforced at data access layer. Federal courts follow CAS IT standards; provincial courts follow provincial systems.

keySecurityRequirements: Publication ban enforcement in court record systems

industries.name: Canadian Armed Forces
industries.frameworkNotes: Classified networks (SNET/DWAN) separated from unclassified. COMSEC device approval by CSE. NATO interoperability requirements. ITSG-33 mandatory. Five Eyes data sharing protocols apply.

keySecurityRequirements: supply chain integrity for weapons systems

industries.name: CSE (Signals Intelligence)
industries.frameworkNotes: Most requirements classified. Public-facing CCCS cloud security guidance published. CCCS vulnerability disclosure coordination. CCCS Annual Threat Report governs GC cyber posture.

industries.name: CSIS (Domestic Intelligence)
industries.frameworkNotes: HUMINT source data has absolute legal protection. Warrant-based collection under CSIS Act. NSIRA oversight. Intelligence community data sharing via classified channels.

auditRequirements: warrant records sealed by court order

industries.name: Defense Contractors
industries.frameworkNotes: PSPC ISP mandatory for all defense contracts. Controlled Goods Program registration required. ITAR compliance for US-origin defense tech. PSPC requires facility security clearance. CITP (Canadian IT security profile) for system classification.

industries.name: DRDC (Defence Research)
industries.frameworkNotes: Research facilities hold facility security clearances. Quantum computing research has additional controls. Export controls on dual-use research. University collaborations require Tech Transfer Agreements.

industries.name: Oil & Gas (Upstream)
industries.frameworkNotes: OT (Operational Technology) networks strictly separated from IT. SCADA security per IEC 62443. Wellbore data subject to provincial Crown royalty reporting. Alberta is Canada's primary upstream hub.

employersByProvince.Alberta: Suncor, CNRL, Cenovus, Imperial Oil, MEG Energy, Baytex (Calgary T1)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: Tourmaline Oil, ARC Resources (Montney play — NE BC)
employersByProvince.Newfoundland: Cenovus Hibernia, ExxonMobil Hebron (St. John's offshore)

P.Eng (APEGA) valued for control systems and process engineering-adjacent roles
NERC CIP cybersecurity training (SANS ICS/SCADA) opens OT security roles
Entry difficulty 3 = Calgary accessible for mid-level DevOps and OT integration engineers
OSIsoft PI (AVEVA) experience is near-universal requirement for data engineering roles

industries.name: Pipelines & Midstream
industries.frameworkNotes: CER Critical Energy Infrastructure protection program. Trans Mountain, Enbridge, TC Energy pipelines under federal oversight. SCADA/DCS systems on isolated OT networks. Geospatial pipeline route data is sensitive.

keySecurityRequirements: cross-border data sharing with US NERC

industries.name: Electricity & Utilities
industries.frameworkNotes: NERC CIP mandatory for bulk electric systems connected to North American grid. Smart meter data is PII under PIPEDA. Ontario IESO and BC Hydro operate under distinct provincial frameworks.

keySecurityRequirements: NERC CIP CIP-002 through CIP-013 controls
auditRequirements: Smart meter data varies by province

industries.name: Nuclear Energy

industries.frameworkNotes: CNSC regulates all Canadian nuclear facilities. Bruce Power, OPG (ON), NB Power Point Lepreau, AECL/CNL. Safety systems on air-gapped networks. Nuclear material accounting reported to IAEA. CANDU reactor-specific cybersecurity requirements.

industries.name: Renewables & Cleantech
industries.frameworkNotes: Grid interconnection requires NERC CIP compliance for bulk systems. Smart inverter protocols (IEEE 2030.5) security requirements. Carbon credit systems subject to PIPEDA and provincial regulations. IESO/AESO market data feeds require secure access.

industries.name: Mining & Resources
industries.frameworkNotes: Geological survey data (seismic, assay) is commercially sensitive IP. TSX/TSX-V material information disclosure rules. Indigenous consultation records may require privacy protections. Automated mine systems follow IEC 62443.

auditRequirements: Geological data retained as trade secret

industries.name: Cloud Services
industries.frameworkNotes: AWS Canada (Central and West), Azure Canada Central/East, GCP Canada provide Canadian data residency. GC Marketplace for PSPC-approved cloud services. CCCS ITSP.50.105 cloud security guidance. GC PBMM required for all federal workloads.

industries.name: Cybersecurity
industries.frameworkNotes: CCCS (Canadian Centre for Cyber Security) is primary guidance body. GC clients require ITSG-33. CCCS Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure guidelines for security research. CCCS Annual Threat Assessment governs national posture.

employersByProvince.Ontario: BlackBerry Security (Waterloo/Toronto), eSentire (Cambridge), Arctic Wolf CA, Cyclera (Toronto)

industries.name: Hardware & Semiconductor
industries.frameworkNotes: Canada's National Quantum Strategy (2022) includes security implications. ITAR/EAR applies to exported goods containing US technology. Quantum hardware research at IQC (Waterloo) and TRIUMF.

auditRequirements: Design IP records retained as trade secret

industries.name: Wireless Carriers (MNO)
industries.frameworkNotes: Big 3: Bell, Rogers, Telus. CRTC-mandated MVNO wholesale access (2021 decision). Lawful interception under Criminal Code Part VI. 5G security guidance from CCCS (ITSAP.80.004). ISED manages spectrum allocation.

industries.name: Internet Service Providers
industries.frameworkNotes: CRTC net neutrality rules (Internet Traffic Management Practices). Mandatory DMARC/DNSSEC deployment guidance. Domain blocking orders (piracy via court order). CASL requires cooperation with CRTC enforcement.

auditRequirements: Traffic logs 1 yr (court order may extend)

industries.name: Broadcasting & Cable
industries.frameworkNotes: Online Streaming Act (C-11, 2023) extends CRTC jurisdiction to streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney+). CanCon requirements mandate % of Canadian programming. National Public Alerting System (NPAS) emergency broadcast mandatory.

industries.name: Satellite Communications
industries.frameworkNotes: Telesat, MDA Group key Canadian players. Radarsat/WCAAF earth observation data may be defense-adjacent. Telesat Lightspeed LEO constellation under development. Canadian Space Agency data sharing protocols.

industries.name: Grocery & Food Retail
industries.frameworkNotes: Loblaws, Metro, Sobeys, Costco. Loyalty programs (PC Optimum, Scene+) subject to PIPEDA consent. CFIA food safety traceability systems (STELA). PIPEDA breach notification if loyalty data compromised.

industries.name: E-Commerce & Marketplace
industries.frameworkNotes: Shopify (Waterloo/Ottawa origin), Amazon.ca, Kijiji. CASL consent mandatory for transactional email. Quebec Law 25 stricter consent requirements than federal PIPEDA. PIPEDA consent for behavioral tracking.

industries.name: General Retail
industries.frameworkNotes: Hudson's Bay Company, Canadian Tire, Dollarama. In-store facial recognition analytics banned under OPC/provincial orders. Quebec Law 25 applies to Quebec retail operations. Loyalty programs under PIPEDA.

industries.name: Automotive Manufacturing
industries.frameworkNotes: Ontario auto corridor: Windsor, Oshawa, Cambridge, Ingersoll. Stellantis, GM, Toyota, Honda plants. Connected vehicle data follows PIPEDA. Autonomous vehicle testing regulated by Transport Canada and Ontario.

industries.name: Aerospace & Aviation
industries.frameworkNotes: Bombardier, Pratt & Whitney Canada, Magellan Aerospace, CAE. DO-178C/DO-326A for avionics software certification. ITAR applies to US-origin aerospace components. Defense contracts require PSPC ISP. Montreal is Canada's aerospace hub.

keySecurityRequirements: ITAR export controls on tech transfer
auditRequirements: DO-178C qualification records retained with aircraft

industries.name: Food Processing & Manufacturing
industries.frameworkNotes: SFCR requires traceability one step back, one step forward. CFIA Preventive Control Plans (PCPs) must be in digital systems. McCain Foods, Maple Leaf Foods, Saputo are key anchors. Allergen management system integrity is critical.

industries.name: Industrial Equipment & Heavy Mfg
industries.frameworkNotes: Finning International, Toromont, CNH Industrial Canada. OT/SCADA for automated production lines. Predictive maintenance IoT creates data obligations. Export of dual-use equipment under Export Controls Order.

industries.name: Broadcasting (TV & Radio)
industries.frameworkNotes: CBC/Radio-Canada, CTV, Global, Corus, Bell Media. CanCon requirements per CRTC. National Public Alerting System (NPAS) emergency broadcast mandatory. French-language programming requirements for QC-distributed content.

industries.name: Video Gaming
industries.frameworkNotes: Montreal gaming hub: Ubisoft, Eidos-Montréal, WB Games, Square Enix. Quebec government game tax credits. Loot box regulation under review in Canada. Quebec Law 25 stricter for QC studios.

industries.name: Film & TV Production
industries.frameworkNotes: Vancouver (BC Film) and Toronto (Ontario Creates) primary hubs. Federal/provincial tax credits (CAVCO, OMDC) require Canadian content certification. MPA content security standards for major studio co-productions. ACTRA/IATSE union data management.

employersByProvince.Quebec: Rodeo FX (Montréal), Framestore Montréal, Lakeside Studio (Montréal)

industries.name: Digital Media & Publishing

auditRequirements: Research data per RDM plan (typically 10 yr)

industries.name: K-12 Schools

industries.name: Online Learning & EdTech
industries.frameworkNotes: D2L (Desire2Learn — Waterloo), Top Hat, Prodigy are Canadian EdTech anchors. CASL consent for course reminders. AODA accessibility compliance mandatory in Ontario. Quebec Law 25 cookie consent for QC learners.

industries.name: Air Transport
industries.frameworkNotes: Air Canada, WestJet, Transat. CATSA for passenger screening. API/PNR data shared with CBSA under CANPASS. Nav Canada air traffic control systems are critical infrastructure. ICAO cybersecurity standards (Doc 10164).

industries.name: Rail Transport
industries.frameworkNotes: CN, CP (CPKC), VIA Rail, commuter rail (GO Transit, RTM). SCADA for wayside signaling and PTC (Positive Train Control). TDG Act for dangerous goods manifests. Critical infrastructure designation for transcontinental rail.

industries.name: Trucking & Logistics
industries.frameworkNotes: ELD (Electronic Logging Device) mandate (2021) creates regulated driver data. CBSA eManifest for cross-border shipments. C-TPAT for Canada-US border trade. PIP (Partners in Protection) program for trusted shippers.

industries.name: Marine, Ports & Shipping
industries.frameworkNotes: Port authorities (Vancouver, Montreal, Halifax, Prince Rupert) designated critical infrastructure. ISPS Code security plans are classified. VTS (Vessel Traffic Services) under Nav Canada/Coast Guard. Arctic sovereignty implications for Northern shipping routes.

industries.name: Urban & Public Transit
industries.frameworkNotes: Presto (Metrolinx-ON), Compass (TransLink-BC), Opus (STM-QC). Tap-in/tap-out creates detailed location history under PIPEDA. AODA accessibility compliance mandatory for Ontario transit apps. TTC, OC Transpo, STM, Calgary Transit, Edmonton Transit.

industries.name: Residential Real Estate
mandatoryCompliance: Prohibition on Purchase by Non-Canadians Act (2022)
industries.frameworkNotes: FINTRAC requires realtors to collect client ID and beneficial ownership (since 2023 amendments). Foreign buyer ban under Prohibition on Purchase by Non-Canadians Act (2022). BC Beneficial Ownership Land Title Registry (LOTR) integration required.

industries.name: Commercial Real Estate
mandatoryCompliance: Provincial real estate and planning acts
industries.frameworkNotes: Cadillac Fairview, Oxford Properties, RioCan. Building automation systems (BAS) require OT security. REIT disclosure rules under CSA National Instruments. Smart building IoT creates new PIPEDA obligations.

industries.name: Property Management
industries.frameworkNotes: Boardwalk, Killam, Minto. Tenant credit checks under provincial tenancy acts. Smart lock and access fob data creates location PII under PIPEDA. Maintenance request data is PII. FINTRAC applies for cash rental transactions.

industries.name: Crop Farming & Grain
mandatoryCompliance: CFIA (Feeds Act, Seeds Act, Plant Protection Act)
industries.frameworkNotes: Canadian Grain Commission regulates grain grading and weighing. Prairie grain belt (SK, MB, AB) is primary region. Precision agriculture platforms (Farmers Edge, Trimble) handle sensitive crop yield/soil data. Provincial farm data legislation emerging (Alberta leading).

industries.name: Livestock & Aquaculture
industries.frameworkNotes: RFID animal traceability via CCIA (Canadian Cattle Identification Agency) for bovine. DFO aquaculture licensing for BC salmon and shellfish. Avian influenza/disease outbreak reporting is mandatory. HACCP plans must be in digital systems.

industries.name: AgriTech & Precision Ag
industries.frameworkNotes: Farmers Edge, Decisive Farming, Veritas Farm Management are key Canadian AgriTech firms. Farm data ownership legislation has no federal law yet (Alberta leading with emerging rules). Satellite/drone imagery creates data sovereignty questions. Saskatoon is Canada's AgriTech hub.

keySecurityRequirements: crop yield prediction model IP protection

industries.name: Law Firms
mandatoryCompliance: Provincial law society rules (LSO-ON, LSBC, BSQ-QC)
industries.frameworkNotes: Solicitor-client privilege creates absolute confidentiality obligation. Law Society of Ontario Technology Practice Area guidelines. FINTRAC reporting for real estate lawyers and trust accounts. Cloud storage requires law society compliance assessment before adoption.

auditRequirements: File retention per law society rules (typically 10 yr)

industries.name: Accounting, Audit & Tax
industries.frameworkNotes: Big 4 Canadian practices (Deloitte, PwC, KPMG, EY). CSQM (Canadian Standard on Quality Management) for firms. CRA e-filing security (EFILE). PCAOB oversight for US-listed Canadian companies. Data analytics in audit creates bulk financial data obligations.

industries.name: Management Consulting
industries.frameworkNotes: McKinsey, Deloitte, PwC, Accenture, KPMG Canadian practices. Government consulting contracts require PSPC security screening (reliability or secret). No sector-specific regulation — inherits client regulatory context.

industries.name: Registered Charities
industries.frameworkNotes: CRA charity registration requires annual T3010 reporting. CASL rules for non-profits relaxed but commercial electronic message rules still apply for fundraising. International fund transfer reporting if political activities exceed 10% threshold.

auditRequirements: Donor PII 7 yr post last donation

industries.name: Healthcare Charities
industries.frameworkNotes: Canadian Cancer Society, Heart & Stroke, MS Society. Patient data subject to PHIPA where circle-of-care adjacent. Research funding requires TCPS 2 alignment. Patient registries under PHIPA. CRA charity rules limit advocacy activities.

industries.name: Indigenous Community Services
industries.frameworkNotes: OCAP® principles: data must be Owned and Controlled by the community. FNIGC (First Nations Information Governance Centre) governs standards. Sixties Scoop and residential school records require special handling. ISC funding agreements require data sharing with consent.

auditRequirements: Band council records per community governance
