envInstructions.text: Build note: ARG PM=pnpm → pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm run build
envInstructions.label: Build note:
envInstructions.instruction: ARG PM=pnpm → pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm run build

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: ESLint + Prettier + Error Lens (VS Code / WebStorm)

stages.command: docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities

phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

envInstructions.instruction: Output served by nginx — no Node.js at runtime

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: ESLint + Prettier + Error Lens (VS Code / WebStorm)

stages.command: docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

envInstructions.label: SCA note:
envInstructions.instruction: Replace npm with yarn audit or pnpm audit per ARG PM

envInstructions.text: Build note: Output served by nginx — no Node.js at runtime
envInstructions.label: Build note:
envInstructions.instruction: Output served by nginx — no Node.js at runtime

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: VS Code + React Native Tools + Flipper

stages.command: npm test && npx detox test --configuration android.emu.release

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.command: docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout

envInstructions.label: SCA note:
envInstructions.instruction: Replace npm with yarn audit or pnpm audit per ARG PM

envInstructions.text: Build note: ARG PM=pnpm → pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm run build
envInstructions.label: Build note:
envInstructions.instruction: ARG PM=pnpm → pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm run build

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation

phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

envInstructions.instruction: Single-binary runtime — no separate build step needed

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Bundle + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: Pylance + Black + Ruff + mypy (VS Code / PyCharm)

stages.command: docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

envInstructions.text: SCA note: Alternative: safety check -r requirements.txt
envInstructions.label: SCA note:
envInstructions.instruction: Alternative: safety check -r requirements.txt

envInstructions.text: Build note: ARG PM=poetry → poetry install --no-dev; ARG PM=uv → uv pip install
envInstructions.label: Build note:
envInstructions.instruction: ARG PM=poetry → poetry install --no-dev; ARG PM=uv → uv pip install

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: IntelliJ IDEA + SonarLint / VS Code Java Extension Pack

stages.command: docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

envInstructions.text: SCA note: Gradle: ./gradlew dependencyCheckAnalyze
envInstructions.label: SCA note:
envInstructions.instruction: Gradle: ./gradlew dependencyCheckAnalyze

envInstructions.text: Build note: ARG BUILD_TOOL=gradle → ./gradlew bootJar; produces fat JAR
envInstructions.label: Build note:
envInstructions.instruction: ARG BUILD_TOOL=gradle → ./gradlew bootJar; produces fat JAR

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period

phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings

phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: Intelephense + PHP_CodeSniffer + VS Code PHP extension

semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only

phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging

phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules

phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider

phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault
